author: mark
text: Cross-check seam (from bl-62bc federation work, 2026-06-03): bl-2e26 §6/§12 introduced `bl plugin install --from <branch>` — the consent-gated onboarding path where a fresh clone adopts a center's RECOMMENDED plugin set (reads the upstream branch's committed <op>/<phase>/ wiring, installs those plugins locally, user-in-the-loop; a center can't run a binary you didn't opt into). This overlaps adopt/disown territory (taking over / detaching from existing remote balls state). Before speccing adopt/disown, reconcile: (a) is 'install --from' a special case of 'adopt', or orthogonal (recommended-plugin-set vs whole-branch-state)? (b) should they share ONE mechanism/verb rather than two — new verbs are a smell? (c) is uninstalling a federated plugin set the natural inverse that 'disown' should cover? Collapse if they're the same act.
